mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue expectations; higher RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month RSAFS growth and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 for two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions; when FEDFUNDS shifts upward, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by at least 0.25 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or the mean triggered return is not below baseline at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and slowdown in payroll growth signals weakening labor income and recession risk; this joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS 3-month growth decelerates
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term has a nonnegative coefficient in walk-forward regressions or triggered 60-day returns are not lower than untriggered returns in at least 60% of expanding windows.

mechanism_narrative: A turn from falling to rising industrial production signals improving real output and earnings breadth; when INDPRO momentum crosses positive, S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change crosses from negative to positive
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO positive-crossing triggers have a median 20-day S&P 500 return less than or equal to the unconditional median in two consecutive 5-year walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in housing starts signal weaker construction activity, durable-goods demand, and credit-sensitive household spending; this housing contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months and is below its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered housing contractions are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations or show no negative excess return versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a rising policy rate compresses equity valuations through both margin pressure and higher discount rates; the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS tightening signal predicts negative 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth rises while FEDFUNDS increases over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the CPIAUCSL by FEDFUNDS interaction coefficient is positive or triggered observations fail to underperform the unconditional 20-day return by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is very high but falling, excess labor slack supports earnings recovery without immediate wage pressure; declining UNRATE from an elevated level predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is above 8% and falls by at least 0.2 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: post-recession labor-market recovery with high slack
failure_condition: Reject if high-slack falling-UNRATE triggers do not produce a higher mean 60-day S&P 500 return than high-slack nonfalling-UNRATE periods at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP together with falling industrial production confirms broad demand and output weakness; this joint recession-pressure signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is negative while INDPRO 3-month change is negative
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 and INDPRO contraction trigger has nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns across walk-forward tests or loses significance after excluding crisis months.
